title: Add more playable and enemy characters to the 3D fighting game

requirements.text: Add at least 2 new distinct enemy character types beyond Sukuna to the 3D fighting game. Each new enemy should have a unique name, distinct 3D character mesh (multi-part articulated body using grouped meshes in Character3D.tsx or a new character component), unique color scheme and emissive material highlights, and its own set of stats (health, speed, attack damage, attack range) defined in the level configuration in frontend/src/types/game.ts. New enemy types should appear in later levels or alongside Sukuna enemies as the levels progress.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 new enemy character types are implemented with unique names (e.g., not 'Sukuna').
Each new enemy has a visually distinct 3D mesh with its own color scheme and materials.
Each new enemy has unique stats (health, speed, damage) defined in the game type/config.
New enemies appear in the game during gameplay (in later levels or mixed with existing enemies).
New enemies use the existing enemy AI logic (moving toward UG, attacking at close range).
Health bars in the HUD correctly reflect the active enemy's name and health.

requirements.text: Add at least 1 new playable character option for the player, beyond UG. The player should be able to select their character from a simple character-select screen shown before the game starts. Each playable character must have a unique name, distinct 3D mesh with unique materials/colors in Character3D.tsx, and slightly different base stats (e.g., speed, health, attack power) reflected in the Character type in frontend/src/types/game.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: A character selection screen is displayed before the game begins with at least 2 playable characters (UG + at least 1 new character).
Each playable character has a unique name and visually distinct 3D mesh.
Each playable character has distinct base stats (health, speed, attack power) in the game types.
The selected character is used as the player character throughout the game session.
The HUD correctly displays the selected player character's name and health bar.
All existing controls (keyboard + on-screen buttons) function correctly for the new playable character(s).

requirements.text: Extend the Character interface and level configuration types in frontend/src/types/game.ts to support multiple character types. Add a 'characterType' string field (or enum) to the Character interface to differentiate between character variants (e.g., 'ug', 'newPlayer', 'sukuna', 'newEnemy1', 'newEnemy2'). Update LEVEL_CONFIGS or equivalent constants to specify which enemy types appear in each level.
acceptanceCriteria: The Character interface includes a 'characterType' field that identifies the character variant.
LEVEL_CONFIGS or equivalent constants specify enemy character types per level.
The TypeScript types compile without errors.
Character3D.tsx uses the characterType field to render the correct mesh for each character.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
All existing keyboard and on-screen touch/click controls must continue to work without functional changes.
Existing game logic (physics, combat system, particle effects, levels, sword, gun) must remain intact.
nonGoals: Backend changes — all new character data is frontend-only.
Adding new weapons or combat mechanics beyond what is needed to support the new characters.
Replacing or removing Sukuna as an enemy character.
